rulings.Case Title: JOSEY RUSSELL VS. BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October 7, 2016, line 1. DEFENDANT RECONTRUST COMPANY, N.A., BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., HSBC BANK USA, N.A., AS TRUSTEE FOR THE HOLDERS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication DENIED. I. As to issue 1 (1st cause of action) moving party failed to shift its burden and (1) Failed to show that Plaintiff was in default on her loan, i.e. (a) that her minimum payments changed from the original payment amount prior to December 2011 (Note paragraph 1, 2(A), 3(B)(i) and (ii), 3(C) and Disclosure Statement). The evidence relied upon for this proposition (Motion 6: 9-11; SS, Issue 1, facts 4 and 7) shows that the change was possible and does not show that the change in fact occurred; or (b) that the escrow amounts have been demanded to be repaid per the terms of the DOT, paragraph 9 ("These amounts shall bear interest at the Note rate from the date of disbursement and shall be payable, with such interest, upon notice from Lender to Borrower requesting payment" ); The evidence relied upon for this proposition (Motion 6:12 "and sought repayment from Russell" and SS issue 1, facts 6 and 13) shows that the demand could have been made, but does not show that it was in fact made. (2) Failed to show that the beneficial interest in the Note and Deed of Trust was "transferred" to Countrywide Financial. Even if the Bailee Letter, which is not purported to be originated or received by Bank of America from the sender in its regular course of business, were admissible evidence, it does not show that Countrywide Financial ever "accepted" it or ever paid the purchased price. Additionally, no evidence is presented to show a connection between Countrywide Financial and Bank of America N.A., as successor by merger to BAC Home Loans Servicing LP FKA Countrywide Home Loans Servicing LP II. As to issue 2 (3rd cause of action) moving party failed to shift its burden and "establish that the loan was transferred in May 2007 (Motion 7:22-23; SS Issue 2, fact 3). See I(2) above. III. As to issue 3 (5th cause of action) moving party fails to shift its burned and show that Plaintiff's minimum payment amount not only could have changed under the contract, but in fact did change (Motion 8:11-17; Issue 3, facts 6, 7, 8, 10, 11) in a way other than indicated in the Note paragraphs 1, 2(A), 3(B)(i) and (ii), 3(C) and Disclosure Statement; or that Plaintiff was notified of the changes per paragraph 4 of the Note; and/or that payment of escrow amount was requested per paragraph 9 of the DOT. IV. As to issues 4 and 5 (2nd cause of action) Defendants argue that summary adjudication is proper because the preceding causes of action are wholly without merit "according [sic], this claim must likewise fail" (Motion 9:3-5) and because Plaintiff failed to submit her contractually required payments (Motion 10:1-2). Since Defendants failed to establish that the other causes of action fail, the Motion for Summary Adjudication as to this cause of action is DENIED. Finally, to the extent Defendants argue that UCL cause of action fails because Plaintiff has not pleaded restitution damages (Motion 10:6), Plaintiff is seeking injunction (Third Amended Complaint 25: 18-21), a remedy available for a violation of B&P 17200. Therefore Motion for Summary Adjudication as to issues 1-5 is DENIED and Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. =(501/SRB)

rulings.Case Title: RAY PARKER GAYLORD VS. DEUTSCHE BANK, NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, AS TRUSTEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-15-654243 WITH CASE CGC-16-549778
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October 7, 2016, line 2. PLAINTIFF RAY GAYLORD MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-15-654243 WITH CASE CGC-16-549778 is OFF CALENDAR. Moving party repeatedly fails to comply with SFLR 2.7B and fails to provide courtesy copies of its filings. See June 22, 2016 Minute order. =(501/SRB)

rulings.Case Title: SANTANA ESG, INC. ET AL VS. IRWIN WELKER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October 7, 2016, line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT UMPQUA BANK DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT by no later than 9:00 a.m. on October 7, 2016 counsel for cross-complainant, Robert Borris, Jr, is ordered to file, serve and provide a courtesy copy of a Declaration explaining his (1) failure to comply with CCP 430.41; (2) failure to comply with CCP 1005; and (3) failure to comply with SF Local Rule 2.7B. =(501/SRB)

rulings.Case Title: 900 SEASIDE SCHOOL LLC VS. JON L HUGHEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October 7, 2016, line 4. DEFENDANT JIAN LUO MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is DENIED. Moving party fails to sustain burden under CCP 473(b). =(501/SRB)

Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October 7, 2016, line 5. DEFENDANT MELISSA SOTTO MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED, moving party failed to show that this UD/Notice based on CCP 1161(4) fails to comply with California Law, i.e. CCP 1161(4). =(501/SRB)

Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October 7, 2016, line 5. DEFENDANT MELISSA SOTTO DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. First Amended Complaint sufficiently alleges a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. =(501/SRB)
